subject: ali-yahya

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized infrastructure / DePIN
sectors_excited.why: Networks that coordinate real-world hardware — compute, storage, wireless, energy, sensors — with token incentives can bootstrap physical supply faster and more cheaply than centralized incumbents. This is the core of what I focus on: using crypto rails to build open, permissionless infrastructure that competes with closed platforms.

sectors_excited.name: Crypto x AI
sectors_excited.why: Coming from a machine-learning background, the intersection is the most exciting frontier to me right now: decentralized compute markets for training and inference, verifiable provenance of data and model outputs, and crypto as a payment/coordination layer for AI resources. As AI concentrates power in a handful of firms, crypto offers credible neutrality and open participation.

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge cryptography
sectors_excited.why: ZK is finally becoming practical — both for scaling (validity/zk rollups) and for privacy and verifiable computation. I treat it as foundational infrastructure for the next phase of the space, not a niche.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized social & digital ownership/identity
sectors_excited.why: Protocols that give users ownership of their social graph, identity, and content — portable across applications rather than locked inside a single platform — are a long-running thesis area I believe in.

sectors_concerned.name: US regulation-by-enforcement
sectors_concerned.why: The absence of clear rules combined with the recent wave of enforcement actions against US exchanges is a structural headwind. My worry is that it drives builders, talent, and capital offshore rather than producing workable policy.

sectors_concerned.name: Centralized intermediaries / CeFi
sectors_concerned.why: The past year's blowups exposed the systemic risk of opaque centralized custodians and reinforced why decentralization and self-custody matter. I'm concerned about continued reliance on trusted intermediaries dressed up as crypto.

sectors_concerned.name: Speculation without utility
sectors_concerned.why: A large share of on-chain activity is still pure speculation detached from real usage. My concern is that it crowds out and discredits the builders doing genuine infrastructure work.

risk_regime.why: As a long-horizon venture investor I'm constructive on building through the downturn — the best companies tend to be built in bear markets, and we keep deploying into early-stage infrastructure regardless of price. But the liquid backdrop in mid-2023 is genuinely risk-off: crypto winter, macro tightening, and a hostile US regulatory climate. Net: cautious on near-term liquid markets, constructive on multi-year technology bets.

notes: I have no prior statements on record in this dataset, so every item here is extrapolated from my role and known focus as an a16z crypto GP — infrastructure, DePIN, and the crypto x AI intersection given my ML background — as of mid-2023, not from dated quotes. Hence no citations and nothing marked grounded or persisted. I operate at the sector/category level rather than making specific token calls, so both token arrays are intentionally empty. Context reflects the post-FTX, SEC-enforcement, crypto-winter environment of June 2023.
